content:
# Joshua Chapter 22 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 246-247 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_022_page_001.jpg`

1. Then Yehoshua called to the Reuvenites and to the Gadites and to the half-tribe of Menasheh,

2. and he said to them, "You have kept all which Moshe, servant of YHWH, commanded you, and you have listened to my voice in all which I commanded you.

3. You have not forsaken your brothers these many days until this day, and you have kept the charge of the commandment of YHWH your God.

4. And now, YHWH your God has given rest to your brothers, as he spoke to them. And now, turn and go to your tents, to the land of your possession, which Moshe, servant of YHWH, gave to you on the other side of the Yarden.

5. Only be very careful to do the commandment and the Torah which Moshe, servant of YHWH, commanded you — to love YHWH your God and to walk in all his ways and to keep his commandments and to cling to him and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul."

6. And Yehoshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.

7. And to the half-tribe of Menasheh, Moshe had given [a portion] in the Bashan, and to its [other] half Yehoshua gave [a portion] with their brothers on the other side of the Yarden, westward. And also when Yehoshua sent them away to their tents, and he blessed them,

8. and he spoke to them, saying, "With much wealth return to your tents, and with very much livestock, with silver and with gold and with bronze and with iron and with very many garments. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers."

9. And the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh returned and went from the sons of Yisrael, from Shiloh which [is] in the land of Kenaan, to go to the land of the Gilad, to the land of their possession in which they had been made to possess, according to the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moshe.

10. And they came to the regions of the Yarden which [are] in the land of Kenaan, and the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh built there an altar by the Yarden — an altar great in appearance.

11. And the sons of Yisrael heard, saying, "Behold, the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh have built the altar toward the face of the land of Kenaan, in the regions of the Yarden, on the side of the sons of Yisrael."

12. And the sons of Yisrael heard, and all the congregation of the sons of Yisrael assembled at Shiloh to go up against them for war.

13. And the sons of Yisrael sent to the sons of Reuven and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Menasheh, to the land of the Gilad, Pinechas son of Elazar the priest,

14. and ten leaders with him — one leader, one leader for [each] father's house for all the tribes of Yisrael; and each one, he [was] head of the house of their fathers among the thousands of Yisrael.

15. And they came to the sons of Reuven and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Menasheh, to the land of the Gilad, and they spoke with them, saying,

16. "Thus says all the congregation of YHWH, 'What [is] this treachery which you have committed against the God of Yisrael, to turn away this day from after YHWH, in that you have built for yourselves an altar, to rebel this day against YHWH?

17. Is [it] too small for us [the] iniquity of Peor, from which we have not cleansed ourselves until this day, and there was the plague in the congregation of YHWH?

18. And you, you turn away this day from after YHWH! And it shall be, you rebel today against YHWH, and tomorrow he will be angry with all the congregation of Yisrael.

19. And surely, if the land of your possession [is] unclean, cross over for yourselves to the land of the possession of YHWH, where the tabernacle of YHWH dwells, and take possession among us. And only against YHWH do not rebel, and against us do not rebel, in your building for yourselves an altar besides the altar of YHWH our God.

20. Did not Akhan son of Zerach commit a treachery in the devoted thing, and upon all the congregation of Yisrael there was wrath? And that man did not perish alone in his iniquity.'"

21. And the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh answered, and they spoke with the heads of the thousands of Yisrael,

22. "El, God, YHWH! El, God, YHWH! He knows! And Yisrael, he shall know! If in rebellion and if in treachery against YHWH — do not save us this day —

23. [if] to build for ourselves an altar to turn away from after YHWH, and if to offer upon it burnt offering and grain offering, and if to make upon it peace offerings — YHWH himself shall seek [it out].

24. And if not, from fear of [this] matter we have done this, saying, 'Tomorrow your sons may speak to our sons, saying, "What [is there] to you and to YHWH, God of Yisrael?

25. And a border YHWH has set between us and between you, sons of Reuven and sons of Gad — the Yarden. There is no portion for you in YHWH." And your sons will cause our sons to cease from fearing YHWH.'

26. And we said, 'Let us now act for ourselves to build the altar — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice —

27. but it [is] a witness between us and between you and between our generations after us, to serve the service of YHWH before him with our burnt offerings and with our sacrifices and with our peace offerings, and your sons will not say to our sons tomorrow, "There is no portion for you in YHWH."'

28. And we said, 'And it shall be, when they say [this] to us and to our generations tomorrow, and we shall say, "See the pattern of the altar of YHWH which our fathers made — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice — but it [is] a witness between us and between you."'

29. Far be it from us to rebel against YHWH and to turn away this day from after YHWH, to build an altar for burnt offering, for grain offering, and for sacrifice, besides the altar of YHWH our God which [is] before his tabernacle."

30. And Pinechas the priest and the leaders of the congregation — and the heads of the thousands of Yisrael who [were] with him — heard the words which the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad and the sons of Menasheh spoke, and it was good in their eyes.

31. And Pinechas son of Elazar the priest said to the sons of Reuven and to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Menasheh, "This day we know that YHWH [is] in our midst, because you have not committed this treachery against YHWH. Then you have delivered the sons of Yisrael from the hand of YHWH."

32. And Pinechas son of Elazar the priest and the leaders returned from the sons of Reuven and from the sons of Gad, from the land of the Gilad, to the land of Kenaan, to the sons of Yisrael, and they brought back word to them.

33. And the word was good in the eyes of the sons of Yisrael, and the sons of Yisrael blessed God. And they did not speak to go up against them for war, to destroy the land in which the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad [were] dwelling.

34. And the sons of Reuven and the sons of Gad called the altar [Ed], for "it [is] a witness between us that YHWH [is] God."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 "on the other side of the Yarden"** — Heb. *be'ever ha-Yarden*. The phrase throughout this chapter carries directional ambiguity depending on the speaker's vantage point; here it refers to the Transjordan (east side).

2. **v.7 "westward"** — Heb. *yammah*, lit. "sea-ward," referring to the Mediterranean as the western reference point.

3. **v.9 "in which they had been made to possess"** — The Hophal form *ho'achazu* indicates passive causation: they were caused to take possession by YHWH's decree through Moshe.

4. **v.10 "an altar great in appearance"** — Heb. *mizbeach gadol le-mar'eh*. The emphasis on its visual impressiveness is what triggered alarm among the western tribes.

5. **v.17 "iniquity of Peor"** — Refers to the incident at Baal Peor (Num 25:1-9) where Yisrael was ensnared into idolatry and a plague killed twenty-four thousand.

6. **v.19 "the tabernacle of YHWH dwells"** — Heb. *shakan mishkan YHWH*, a wordplay: the root *sh-k-n* (to dwell) with its cognate noun *mishkan* (dwelling-place/tabernacle). The tabernacle was at Shiloh at this time.

7. **v.20 "Akhan son of Zerach"** — Cf. Joshua 7. The LC reads *Akhan* (עכן) here, not *Akar* (עכר) as in 1 Chr 2:7.

8. **v.22 "El, God, YHWH! El, God, YHWH!"** — Heb. *El Elohim YHWH, El Elohim YHWH*. A threefold divine invocation repeated twice, forming a solemn oath formula. The three divine names are stacked without conjunctions in the Hebrew.

9. **v.24 "from fear of [this] matter"** — Heb. *mi-d'agah mi-davar*, lit. "from anxiety, from a matter/word." The preposition *min* is causal: their motivation was preventive concern, not rebellion.

10. **v.27 "to serve the service of YHWH"** — Heb. *la'avod et-avodat YHWH*, cognate accusative construction intensifying the concept of service/worship.

11. **v.28 "the pattern of the altar"** — Heb. *tavnit mizbach YHWH*. The word *tavnit* (pattern, model, replica) is the same term used for the tabernacle blueprints (Exod 25:9), suggesting the altar was understood as a symbolic replica, not a functional rival.

12. **v.34 "[Ed]"** — The name *Ed* (עד, "Witness") appears in the MT/LC text as the name assigned to the altar. Some manuscripts and versions omit the actual name; the LC preserves it. The explanatory clause "it [is] a witness between us that YHWH [is] God" functions as an etymological gloss.

13. **General note on conjunctions** — The Hebrew waw-consecutive (ו) dominates the narrative structure of this chapter. Every verse-initial "And" reflects the Hebrew *wayyiqtol* or *waw* + noun construction faithfully preserved in this translation.
